meta.description: Eval prompt catalog for access function system prompt update
meta.notes: Run count: prompts.length + sum(triple ? 2 : 0). Workflow computes this from the data.

prompts.prompt: Create an event schedule app where you add acts with stage and time, and star your favorites. Include a text area to paste and parse any schedule.

I'm organizing a music festival. I need a schedule where my crew adds the acts and stage times, but everyone at the event can browse and star their favorites. Only my crew should be able to change the lineup.
Take my photo with the camera, ask me my dream job, then generate a fun caricature of me doing that job. Post it to a public gallery so everyone can see each other's dream jobs.
Take my photo, ask my dream job, make a caricature of me doing it. Post it to a wall where everyone at the party can see all the portraits, but you can only make your own.
Create a task tracker with freeform textarea entry, that sends the text to AI to create task list items using json, and tag them into the selected list.
I need a task dump where I type a mess of thoughts and it sorts them into lists. My team should see the lists I share with them, but my private brain dumps stay mine.

Image auto-tagger app that automatically saves, analyzes, tags, and describes images, displaying them in a masonry grid as soon as they're dropped on the page, adding tags and descriptions as they come back.
Shared photo board for our trip — anyone in the group drops photos and they get auto-tagged, but only I can remove stuff or reorganize the albums.
Chat with historical legends — pick a figure and have a conversation. Results are streamed live.
History chat room where each person in our study group picks a historical figure to talk to, and we can all read each other's wild conversations.
Describe your mood and AI curates the perfect playlist with YouTube search links for each track.
Party playlist where guests describe their mood and get song suggestions, but only the DJ decides which ones make the final queue. Everyone sees the queue.
Paste two people's availability and AI instantly finds the best overlapping times to meet.
Group scheduling where each person pastes their own availability privately, and only the organizer sees the full picture and picks the time.
Pomodoro timer with multiple concurrent timers, work/break intervals, and session stats. Persists across page refreshes.
Team focus board — we each have our own pomodoro running, and everyone can see who's heads-down right now.
Game show trivia — pick any topic, AI generates questions and judges your answers. Styled like a retro board game.
Trivia game night — the host picks topics and AI writes the questions. Players submit answers privately, and only the host reveals the scores at the end.
Flashcard app — pick any topic and AI generates a study deck you can flip through and shuffle.
Study group flashcards — anyone in the group creates decks on different topics, but each person tracks their own progress and scores privately.
AI recipe generator that uses emoji for ingredients. An AI food critic tastes your creations and roasts them with scores.
Cooking club where members post emoji recipes, everyone votes and the AI food critic roasts them. Only the club leader can pin the winner of the week.
Kanban board with buttery drag-and-drop across To Do, Doing, and Done columns.
Team task board where everyone drags their own cards between columns, but only the project lead can create new columns or archive old ones.
Meeting notes app — bullet-point capture during the call, AI summary at the end.
Meeting notes where everyone on the call adds bullet points live, but only the meeting organizer can publish the AI summary afterward.
Daily journal with a mood picker and AI-generated reflection prompts to end your day.
My journal is totally private — only I can see my entries. But I want to be able to share a specific entry with someone if I choose to.
Habit streak tracker — daily check-ins, flame streaks, and a gentle nudge when you slip.
Accountability group where we each track our own habits and streaks, and everyone can cheer each other on but nobody messes with your check-ins.
Bookmark vault — save links, auto-tag by topic, search everything instantly.
Team bookmark library where anyone adds links and they get auto-tagged, but only curators can organize them into collections. Everyone browses everything.
Goal tracker with milestones, a visual progress bar, and celebratory animations on completion.
Team goals where the lead sets the milestones and everyone posts updates on their piece. Only the lead marks milestones complete.
Pixel art editor with a 16×16 grid, custom palette, and export to PNG.
Collaborative pixel art where we each paint on our own layer and the final piece combines everything together.
Soundboard with 16 neon pads that ripple when tapped — load your own sounds or use built-in kits.
Shared soundboard for our podcast — anyone on the team records samples, but only the producer picks which ones go on the board.
Snake with arrow keys, a growing neon tail, and a high-score board that persists.
Snake game where everyone's high scores show up on a shared leaderboard.

Contact page for my landscaping business. Visitors fill out their name, phone number, and what they need done — they don't have to sign up or anything. I get a list of all the requests so I can call them back.

Customer feedback survey — visitors answer 5 questions and submit. They can't see other people's answers or go back and change theirs. My team reviews all the responses and I can publish a summary when we're ready.

Fence quote calculator. The visitor picks fence style, enters their yard measurements, and gets a rough estimate. When they're happy with it they submit a request with their phone number. My sales team gets the request with all the details so they can follow up.

Sign-up sheet for my kid's soccer team. Parents put their name and which games they can bring snacks for. Everyone sees the full schedule but you can only change your own sign-ups. I'm the coach so I can edit anything.

Maintenance request form for our apartment building. Tenants describe the problem and which unit they're in. The building manager sees all the requests and marks them in progress or done. Tenants only see their own.
Simple job application page. Candidates fill in their name, email, and a short cover letter. They can't go back and edit after submitting. Our hiring team reviews all the applications together.
